rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 23, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MURPHY PROPERTIES INC.'s Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Enforce Terms Of Settlement Agreement And For Recovery Of Attorneys' Fees And Expenses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TIBOR SZABO VS. MICHAEL VLASTONE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 23, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT HENRY LAUGHLIN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant maintains his initial burden of production based on (inter alia) his declaration. The court does not entertain plaintiff's untimely opposition. In addition, the court grants the motion because plaintiff failed to file an opposing separate statement. See CCP 437c(b)(3). Lastly, defendant properly objects to plaintiff's "evidence" as conclusory allegations. Plaintiff failed to create a triable issue of material fact because he does not present evidentiary facts to oppose the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL SECURITY SYSTEMS, LLC VS. INTEL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff National Security Syestems Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Intel Corporation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, May 23 2014, Line 5, Plaintiff National Security Systems' Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Intel Corporation Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff National Security Systems, LLC's motion to compel the deposition of defendant Intel Corp. is DENIED. Plaintiff's notice sought to take the deposition of Defendant on a date, May 13, 2014, that was less than 30 days prior to the trial date of June 2, 2014. Under the Code, a deposition is deemed completed on the day it begins, see CCP § 2024.010, and the Code requires that discovery proceedings be completed 30 days prior to the date initially set for trial, see CCP § 2024.020(a). Though a court may grant leave for a party to complete discovery proceedings closer to the initial trial date or to reopen discovery after a new trial date has been set, see CCP § 2024.050(a), the Judge Pro Tem does not construe the Court's order of March 4, 2014 as granting Plaintiff leave to take depositions less than 30 days before the reset trial date of June 2, 2014 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA MONTOYA VS. BRANNAN STREET FITNESS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 23, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT BRANNAN STREET FITNESS, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted (CCP § 437c). Defendant has met its initial burden by demonstrating that Plaintiff accepted an express waiver and assumption of risk in writing. (Knight v. Jewett (1992) 3 Cal.4th 296, 325 (written words are an express assumption of risk. See UMF no. 27; Winchester Decl., Exhibit C.) In turn, Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the waiver should be invalidated. First, the language is neither unclear nor unambiguous. (Winchester Decl., Exhibit C.) Second, Defendant did not engage in gross negligence. (City of Santa Barbara v. Sup.Ct. (2007) 41 Cal.4th 747, 754.) Plaintiff's evidence to show that Defendant did not properly inspect, maintain or keep maintenance records resulting in gross negligence does not create a triable issue of fact. The objections to Baumgarten Declaration are sustained. The Court did not consider the evidence proffered in reply. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: YOSHABEL CLEMENTS VS. HARCOURT GROUP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sojourn Properties Inc. And Steven King'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Document Requests, Set One, And Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary And/Or Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 23, 2014, Line 10, Defendant Sojourn Properties Inc. And Steven King'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Document Requests, Set One, And Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary And/Or Terminating Sanctions. Denied. Neither the motion to compel further responses to Requests for Production, Set One and Special Interrogatories, Set One are timely. (CCP §§ 2030.300(c); 2031.310(c).) On December 26, 2013, Plaintiff served further verified responses to Requests for Production, Set One and Special Interrogatories, Set One. No extension to the time to file a motion to compel further responses were granted. On April 1, 2014, Defendants filed their motion to compel, which is untimely. For Form Interrogatories, Set One, Plaintiff agreed to file supplemental verified responses by May 9, 2014. Monetary sanctions of $ 1,800.00 are awarded in favor of Plaintiff Yoshabel Clements and against Defendants Sojourn Properties, Inc. and Steven G. King and their counsel, jointly and severally, to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL A. TRUFANT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 23, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend. The Second Amended Complaint fails to allege a cause of action for disability discrimination because plaintiff does not allege sufficient facts showing that she is disabled within the meaning of the ADA. The 42 USC 1983 civil rights claims fail to state a cause of action because plaintiff fails to allege that her rights were violated as the result of a CCSF policy or custom. See Monell v. Dept. of Social Services of City of New York, 436 U.S. 658, 691 (1978). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)
